config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang jelas agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat keseimbangan, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar secara mudah. Dalam praktiknya, kalau hak individu terlalu lemah, yang paling sering dirugikan justru kelompok kecil atau minoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take blanket priority over the individual’s rights. From a United States perspective, that idea makes me wary because our history shows how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuses against minorities, dissenters, and unpopular groups. Society absolutely has legitimate interests in public safety, health, and order, but those interests should be limited by strong individual rights, not placed above them by default. When the balance is wrong, the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay for it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya justru melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, persekusi minoritas, atau kebijakan yang merugikan orang kecil. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas, karena tanpa itu masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi alat bagi yang paling kuat. Bagi saya, yang benar bukan menempatkan salah satunya di atas yang lain, melainkan memastikan kepentingan bersama dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, that principle is dangerous because history shows how quickly “the public good” can become a justification for censorship, discrimination, or abuse of minorities. Public safety and social order matter, but they have to be constrained by firm rights, not allowed to override them whenever officials claim it is convenient. To me, a society is strongest when it protects individual liberty first and only limits it in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat semboyan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan kritik, membungkam minoritas, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang tidak adil bagi orang kecil. Kepentingan publik memang perlu, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang kuat; tanpa itu, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat mudah berubah menjadi kepentingan pihak yang paling berkuasa. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan orang per orang sambil tetap mengurus kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that framing sets off alarm bells because our history is full of examples where “the public good” was used to justify censorship, segregation, forced conformity, and other abuses against people with less power. Public safety and shared needs are real, but they should be narrow exceptions with strong limits, not a default permission slip to override basic liberties. In my view, a society earns legitimacy by protecting individuals first, especially when the individual is unpopular, vulnerable, or in the minority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
